mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of stable employment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and quarterly GDP growth confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and GDPC1 both rise, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 20-day SPX returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic activity and consumer spending power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325.0, persistent price pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 325.0, or the inflation threshold loses predictive accuracy during supply-driven inflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales expenditures indicate synchronized strength in durable goods and consumer discretionary demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and RSAFS signal produces negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when housing inventory levels are high.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal becomes positive when accompanied by simultaneous fiscal stimulus announcements.
